head: fc3f0d1
summary: Error catalog contract is intact end-to-end: drift-detect is green (44 catalog / 34 Rust phase-1-required / 44 TS generated); every known error variant across 7 Rust error enums routes through a stable ErrorCode with a documented catalog code; napi bridge prefixes the code into the message so the TS mapNativeError regex can reconstruct typed subclasses; production-refuses-NoAuth is guarded by ProductionRequiresCaps with a clear fix hint in its Display string. However, there are 10 concrete findings — 3 of which materially weaken the source-chain / code-stability contract. No panic paths in production source; all unwrap/expect calls are either in #[cfg(test)] modules, test-only helpers, or locally-justified invariant guards. No Display strings are empty at the variant level.
scope_covered: crates/benten-graph/src/lib.rs (GraphError enum + 6 redb From impls + code())
crates/benten-eval/src/primitives/*.rs (error-edge routing: ON_NOT_FOUND / ON_CONFLICT / ON_DENIED / ON_ERROR / ON_LIMIT / ON_EMPTY)
crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs (EngineError enum + error_code() + code() + static_for + PrimitiveHost round-trip)
packages/engine/src/errors.ts + errors.generated.ts (TS typed classes + mapNativeError + extractCode)
contract_checks_passing: Every Rust error enum exposes a `.code()` fn returning ErrorCode (CoreError, GraphError, CapError, ViewError, EvalError via InvariantViolation, EngineError, RegistrationError)
ErrorCode::as_str / from_str round-trip cleanly via tests/error_codes.rs with Unknown(String) fallback for forward-compat
drift-detect.ts passes live — catalog 44 / Rust 34 / TS 44 all aligned to the phase-gated contract
napi boundary engine_err preserves the catalog code as a message prefix so `extractCode` regex `\bE_[A-Z0-9_]+\b` can reconstruct the typed subclass
packages/engine/src/errors.ts `mapNativeError` has a non-throwing fallback to a synthetic BentenError so an unrecognized code never degrades to an untyped Error

Error-edge routing is consistent: READ fires ON_NOT_FOUND/ON_EMPTY/ON_DENIED; WRITE fires ON_CONFLICT/ON_DENIED; CALL fires ON_DENIED/ON_LIMIT/ON_ERROR; capability-denial on READ routes via ON_DENIED with CapError::DeniedRead payload (not ON_NOT_FOUND — the named compromise #2 honest-existence-leak posture is honoured)
ProductionRequiresCaps Display string is informative and actionable: calls out `.production()` + `.without_caps()` as mutually exclusive and names the fix
CapError::NotImplemented Display string names the backend AND the landing phase AND the interim alternative (NoAuthBackend) — excellent DX for operator misconfiguration

No panic paths in production source. All unwrap/expect calls audited: (a) within #[cfg(test)] modules; (b) in `testing_insert_privileged_fixture` with explicit allow(clippy::expect_used) + reason; (c) locally-justified `stack.last_mut().expect("just pushed")` in invariant walkers where the push happened one line up; (d) mutex-poison recovery via `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())` which is a standard poisoning-tolerant pattern
No Display string is structurally empty — every #[error(...)] template carries at least a literal noun ("capability revoked", "read denied", etc.)

findings.title: EvalError::{Backend, Graph, Core} collapse to ErrorCode::Unknown("") — ambiguous code + empty string
findings.detail: The `EvalError::code()` match arm `EvalError::Backend(_) | EvalError::Graph(_) | EvalError::Core(_) => ErrorCode::Unknown(String::new())` collapses three distinct error sources (host backend, graph layer, core) into a single untyped marker whose stored string is empty. Downstream consumers calling `code().as_str()` on these variants will receive `""` (not even `"E_UNKNOWN"`), which violates the R1 contract that every error surfaces a stable code. The correct mapping is `EvalError::Graph(g) => g.code()` and `EvalError::Core(c) => c.code()` — both inner enums already have a `.code()` fn. `EvalError::Backend(_)` is the genuinely-fallback case and should carry `ErrorCode::Unknown("E_BACKEND")` consistently (matching what `eval_error_to_engine_error` at engine/lib.rs:2145-2148 already does). Multiple variants mapping to the same empty code is also ambiguous — a consumer cannot tell a backend-layer failure from a graph-layer failure from a core-layer failure.
findings.fix: Dispatch to the inner `.code()` for Graph and Core; keep Backend on a distinct `ErrorCode::Unknown("E_BACKEND")` marker.
findings.test_coverage: Contract should land a proptest in benten-eval/tests/error_codes.rs asserting that `EvalError::code().as_str()` is non-empty for every variant (including struct variants populated with test payload).

findings.title: Engine PrimitiveHost impl stringifies EngineError into EvalError::Backend(format!("{e:?}")), dropping source chain and code
findings.detail: The engine's `impl PrimitiveHost for Engine` wraps every `EngineError` returned by `benten-graph` / `benten-caps` via `.map_err(|e| benten_eval::EvalError::Backend(format!("{e:?}")))`. This: (a) stringifies the error via Debug formatter, losing the #[source] chain `std::error::Error::source()` walks; (b) discards the stable ErrorCode; (c) produces a Debug representation (with nested `Redb("redb error: ...")` escaping) that is caller-unfriendly. The reciprocal `eval_error_to_engine_error` at line 2140 then round-trips `Backend(m)` back into `EngineError::Other { code: ErrorCode::Unknown("E_BACKEND"), message: m }` — so a `CapError::Denied` that transits this path emerges on the other side with code `E_BACKEND`, not `E_CAP_DENIED`. A caller catching for `E_CAP_DENIED` sees nothing.
findings.fix: Extend `EvalError` with typed variants mirroring `PrimitiveHost` failures (Cap / Graph / Core — `EvalError::Capability` already exists via From<CapError>; add From<GraphError> and From<CoreError> if not already present, or route through those). The host impl should return `EvalError::Capability(c)` directly on CapError denial rather than format-stringifying the wrapping EngineError. `eval_error_to_engine_error` already handles these inner-typed variants correctly (lines 2142-2144); the typed round-trip will then preserve the original ErrorCode end-to-end.
findings.test_coverage: Add an integration test: register a handler that writes a Node, configure `GrantBackedPolicy` with no matching grant, invoke `engine.call(...)`, assert the returned `EngineError::error_code()` is `ErrorCode::CapDenied` — not `Unknown("E_BACKEND")`.

findings.title: All 6 redb From impls on GraphError stringify, dropping std::error::Error::source() chain
findings.location: crates/benten-graph/src/lib.rs:127-156 (six impls: redb::Error, DatabaseError, TransactionError, TableError, StorageError, CommitError)
findings.detail: Each `impl From<redb::X> for GraphError { fn from(e) -> Self { GraphError::Redb(e.to_string()) } }` converts the redb error into an opaque String. The `#[error("redb: {0}")] Redb(String)` variant has no `#[source]` annotation because there is no inner source to annotate — the chain was broken at the From boundary. The code-catalog mapping `GraphError::Redb(_) | Decode(_) => ErrorCode::Unknown("graph_internal")` (line 111-113) makes this worse: not only is the chain gone, the catalog code is a lowercase-marker string, not a real catalog code. This is already tracked as R4b tech-debt (documented in the Redb variant's docstring), but the R6 observation stands: it is a real gap today — consumers lose the underlying redb error kind (e.g. distinguishing corruption from disk-full from lock contention) and the catalog contract is broken ("graph_internal" is not a valid catalog code and will never match `CODE_TO_CTOR` in the TS mapper).
findings.fix: The deferred refactor is to add `#[from] redb::Error` (and siblings) with a typed inner-error wrapper, flowing `Error::source()` through. Since redb does re-export the variants needed, a less-invasive interim step is to add a new `GraphError::RedbSourced { msg: String, #[source] source: Arc<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync> }` variant and route new code through it, keeping the existing String-carrying variant for test-fixture injection. As an immediate minimal fix: replace `ErrorCode::Unknown("graph_internal")` with a documented catalog code — either reserve `E_GRAPH_INTERNAL` in docs/ERROR-CATALOG.md (and add to the Rust enum and TS generator) or route Redb / Decode through `ErrorCode::Unknown("E_GRAPH_INTERNAL")` so the uppercase-E convention holds.
findings.test_coverage: Add a test that a fabricated `GraphError::Redb("simulated".into())` has `.code().as_str()` matching the `^E_[A-Z0-9_]+$` pattern and is present in the TS codegen's `CATALOG_CODES`.

findings.title: EngineError variants map to `ErrorCode::Unknown(...)` with fabricated codes that aren't in the catalog
findings.detail: Five EngineError variants wrap catalog-looking codes inside `ErrorCode::Unknown(String)`: `E_DUPLICATE_HANDLER`, `E_NO_CAPABILITY_POLICY_CONFIGURED`, `E_PRODUCTION_REQUIRES_CAPS`, `E_SUBSYSTEM_DISABLED`, `E_UNKNOWN_VIEW`, `E_NOT_IMPLEMENTED`. None of these codes are present in docs/ERROR-CATALOG.md. The drift detector currently misses them because `Unknown` variants bypass the `ErrorCode::as_str` enumeration; the parser only sees the `=> "E_XXX"` match arms in error_code.rs, and these codes are synthesized at runtime in engine/lib.rs. So the catalog and the Rust surface disagree silently on these six codes — a TS caller running `mapNativeError` on one of them will fall through to the synthetic `E_UNKNOWN` path because `CODE_TO_CTOR` has no entry.
findings.fix: Reserve the six codes in docs/ERROR-CATALOG.md, regenerate errors.generated.ts (codegen picks them up), and promote them from `ErrorCode::Unknown(String)` to first-class `ErrorCode` variants in error_code.rs. The drift-detector will then enforce parity automatically.
findings.test_coverage: drift-detect.ts + the existing G8 lint will flag additions once the Rust enum catches up.

findings.title: ViewError::PatternMismatch maps to E_INV_REGISTRATION — a runtime-query shape error using a registration-time code
findings.detail: ViewError::PatternMismatch fires at READ time when a caller queries a view with a filter pattern the view doesn't maintain. Mapping it to `ErrorCode::InvRegistration` (E_INV_REGISTRATION, registration-time structural catch-all per catalog) is documented in the source as a temporary choice until `E_IVM_PATTERN_MISMATCH` is reserved in Phase 2. But today this is a layer-violation: a runtime error uses a registration-time code, and a TS caller catching EInvRegistration will get false-positive hits on IVM pattern-mismatches. The comment at view.rs:74-82 acknowledges the gap.
findings.fix: Reserve `E_IVM_PATTERN_MISMATCH` in docs/ERROR-CATALOG.md as a Phase-1 code (it's a real runtime error today, not a Phase-2 deferral), wire it through ErrorCode in error_code.rs, update the TS codegen. The single-line fix inside view.rs then drops the comment.
findings.test_coverage: A view-pattern-mismatch test would today incorrectly assert on EInvRegistration; migrate to the new code once reserved.

findings.title: CapError::Denied Display uses `{required:?}` / `{entity:?}` (Debug-formatted strings with quotes)
findings.detail: The Display string is `capability denied (required={required:?}, entity={entity:?})`. Using `:?` on `String` wraps the value in escaped double-quotes — a denial for scope `store:post:write` on entity `alice` renders as `capability denied (required="store:post:write", entity="alice")` (with literal quotes in the rendered Display output). Once the message travels through `napi_err` it appears in thrown JS Error messages with those escaped quotes intact. Minor readability hit; worse, it breaks regex matches on the contract form. Phase 3 sync logs will also have the quote-pollution.
findings.fix: Change template to `capability denied (required={required}, entity={entity})` — use `:` (Display) instead of `:?` (Debug) on `String`. No type changes needed.
findings.test_coverage: Assert the rendered Display has no literal `\"` sequence when both fields are non-empty.

findings.title: GraphError::BackendNotFound leaks filesystem path via path.display()
findings.detail: The Display template `#[error("backend not found at path: {}", path.display())]` surfaces the full PathBuf into the Display string — absolute path, home directory, everything. This then travels across the napi boundary into JS Error.message and into the thrown BentenError's message. For multi-user or sandbox-shared-log contexts this leaks internal state (home directory name reveals username on macOS/Linux, app-data layout reveals engine version / install form). Typical request: scrub to the filename only, or include only the path the builder was constructed with.
findings.fix: Render `path.file_name().unwrap_or(path.as_os_str()).to_string_lossy()` (or simply omit the path from the Display string and keep it only in the structured context field). Keep the full PathBuf in the struct variant for programmatic introspection; the Display rendering is where the leak happens.
findings.test_coverage: Assert the Display string of a `BackendNotFound { path: "/Users/me/secret/benten.redb" }` does not contain `/Users/me/secret`.

findings.title: napi core_err fabricates E_CORE: prefix — not a catalog code
findings.detail: `core_err` produces a napi Error with message `format!("E_CORE: {err}")` — `E_CORE` is not a catalog code. Compare to `engine_err` which uses `err.code()` (which returns a real `"E_*"` string from `static_for`). The `mapNativeError` TS regex `\bE_[A-Z0-9_]+\b` will happily match `E_CORE` and then fall through to the synthetic-code branch because `CODE_TO_CTOR` has no entry — degrading the typed-error contract. If the napi bridge ever has a code path that raises a raw `CoreError` (rather than going through `EngineError::Core(_)`), the TS caller loses the typed subclass.
findings.fix: Route through `err.code()` the same way engine_err does: `napi::Error::new(Status::InvalidArg, format!("{}: {err}", static_for(&err.code())))`. A helper that wraps both in the same format would prevent future drift.
findings.test_coverage: Vitest assertion: a CoreError-originated thrown error is mapped to the specific ECoreXxx subclass, not to the fallback synthetic wrapper.

findings.title: CapError::DeniedRead carries no structured context — messaging asymmetry with CapError::Denied
findings.detail: `CapError::Denied` is a struct variant carrying `required: String, entity: String` for audit pipelines. `CapError::DeniedRead` is a unit variant with Display `"read denied"` — no `required`, no `entity`, no target-label. The `GrantBackedPolicy::check_read` callsite at grant_backed.rs:185 has the `ctx.label` and the derived `scope` in scope but discards them. Downstream audit/DX consumers who catch `ECapDeniedRead` in TS will see `message: "E_CAP_DENIED_READ: read denied"` and nothing else. The docs/ERROR-CATALOG.md `E_CAP_DENIED_READ` entry explicitly promises a `{ required, entity, target }` context shape (line 130), which the Rust side does not populate.
findings.fix: Promote `DeniedRead` to a struct variant matching `Denied { required: String, entity: String }`. The callsite in grant_backed.rs::check_read passes the derived scope and the label; auditing is preserved.
findings.test_coverage: Update tests/read_denial.rs to assert the structured payload is present on the returned error.

findings.title: static_for in benten-engine duplicates ErrorCode::as_str — single-source-of-truth risk
findings.detail: `fn static_for(c: &ErrorCode) -> &'static str` lifts the same 31 match arms that already exist in `ErrorCode::as_str`. The duplication is explicitly defended in a doc comment (the `'static` vs `&str` lifetime delta), but a drift risk remains: if ErrorCode gains a variant, both match arms must stay aligned or static_for silently returns `"E_UNKNOWN"` for a real code. There's no compile-time exhaustiveness check coupling them.
findings.fix: Either (a) add a second method `ErrorCode::as_static_str(&self) -> &'static str` directly in benten-core that the engine imports — removing the duplication — or (b) add a #[test] in benten-engine that iterates over every `ErrorCode` variant and asserts `static_for(&v) == v.as_str()` to catch drift at CI time. Option (a) is preferred.
findings.test_coverage: drift-detect.ts will NOT catch this because both static_for and as_str emit the same literals into the source text — the drift would silently revert to `E_UNKNOWN`.

findings.title: VersionError has no .code() method — only enum that lacks one
findings.detail: Every other error enum in the workspace exposes `.code() -> ErrorCode` (CoreError, GraphError, CapError, ViewError, InvariantViolation, EngineError). `VersionError` does not. The conversion to a stable code happens one level up — `CoreError::VersionBranched` handles the `Branched` variant, but `VersionError::UnknownPrior` has no mapping at all and will surface as whatever the outer wrapper says. If `append_version` is ever called at the napi boundary (currently it isn't), there's no stable code to forward.
findings.fix: Add `impl VersionError { pub fn code(&self) -> ErrorCode { match self { Branched {..} => VersionBranched, UnknownPrior {..} => NotFound } } }` and document that UnknownPrior falls under E_NOT_FOUND (the caller's prior head does not exist in the chain = the named thing is not found).
findings.test_coverage: Add tests/version_error_codes.rs asserting both variants produce non-Unknown codes.

recommendations_priority_order: r6-err-2 (MAJOR): fix before any production path calls through PrimitiveHost — otherwise cap-denial error codes silently become E_BACKEND for TS callers
r6-err-1 (MAJOR): fix in the same PR as r6-err-2 since they share the EvalError surface
r6-err-3 (MAJOR): either reserve E_GRAPH_INTERNAL in the catalog now (5-min fix) or land the #[from] redb::Error refactor as originally planned for R4b
r6-err-4 (MINOR): reserve the six engine-synthesized codes in the catalog and promote to ErrorCode variants — closes a silent drift in the 3-source contract
r6-err-8 (MINOR): align napi core_err with engine_err (one-line fix)
r6-err-9 (MINOR): promote DeniedRead to a struct variant (R1 triage #2 declared-and-checked contract calls for the structured payload)
r6-err-6 (MINOR): drop the :? Debug-format in CapError::Denied Display template
r6-err-7 (MINOR): scrub PathBuf in GraphError::BackendNotFound Display
r6-err-10 (NIT): deduplicate static_for via a benten-core as_static_str
notes: Pattern audit: the dominant pattern across the workspace is Rust-error enum with a sibling `.code() -> ErrorCode` method. This is a good pattern — it keeps the catalog mapping local to each error enum. The weak points are at cross-crate boundaries (eval ↔ engine, napi ↔ engine) where the mapping is reconstructed rather than forwarded.

Forensic observation: every finding here is a contract-weakness at a boundary. The per-crate error surfaces are individually clean; the cross-crate round-trips lose information. This is a characteristic pattern in workspace-sized Rust projects — worth documenting as an antipattern for Phase 2 agents.

Strength: the drift-detect script IS the kind of cross-source contract enforcement that catches silent decay. It currently runs on catalog ↔ Rust ↔ TS but not on internal Rust ↔ Rust (the static_for / as_str duplication); extending it would be high-leverage.

Strength: the CapError::NotImplemented message is exemplary — it names the backend, the landing phase, and the interim alternative. Every 'feature-not-yet-implemented' error in the workspace should follow this template.

No panic paths in production source code. Every `unwrap()` / `expect()` in src/ is either in #[cfg(test)], in a test-helper gated behind `feature = "test-helpers"`, or locally-justified in invariant walkers where the push/pop pairing is one line apart.

The transaction / error-edge semantics are consistently respected: `E_WRITE_CONFLICT` is routed via ON_CONFLICT (not thrown); capability denial on READ routes ON_DENIED; ITERATE overrun routes ON_LIMIT. The routing contract is honoured symmetrically in READ / WRITE / CALL / ITERATE executors.
